_about: Final-target expert adjudications by claude-code-expert for real, sanitized pilot messages that lacked independent-worker consensus (single label, no labels, or low agreement) or were otherwise ambiguous. These are an ADDITIVE final-target layer; worker MessageLabel rows are never modified. Values use the app's controlled vocabularies (lib/constants.ts). This file contains no raw message bodies, addresses, or secrets. Two reviewable-but-unusable candidates were intentionally excluded as gibberish / an incomplete fragment and are recorded in `_excluded`.

_excluded.reason: gibberish keyboard mash ('12wflekfwfijweofijwe'); no analyzable content
incomplete one-line conversational fragment with no link, request, or context; insufficient to adjudicate

adjudications.recommended_action: Treat this as a promotional email. If you want a loan, open the offer inside the official HDFC Bank app or by typing the bank's website yourself rather than tapping email links.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This looks like a genuine pre-approved loan marketing email from HDFC Bank. It does not ask for passwords or payment, but as a habit, check any offer directly in your bank's app instead of trusting email links.
adjudications.rationale: Sender, reply-to and return-path all resolve to HDFC's real mailer domain with SPF and DMARC passing, links stay on that same domain, and nothing asks for credentials or money; this is a legitimate marketing message, so the safest defensible label is low rather than the split safe/medium worker guesses.

adjudications.recommended_action: Do not click links in the email. Search for the named class-action settlement and file through the official court-appointed settlement administrator's site you find independently.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This reads like a real class-action settlement notice with a claim ID and PIN. It is probably legitimate, but verify it by searching for the official settlement website yourself before entering any details.
adjudications.rationale: The content matches the format of a genuine Google Assistant privacy class-action notice (court-directed language, unique claim ID and PIN); the sender could not be verified, so low with advice to confirm via the official administrator is more defensible than calling it safe or a scam.

adjudications.recommended_action: Do not click the link. Delete the message and block the sender.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is an unsolicited text with a vague come-on and a random, obscure web link. Do not tap it — links like this commonly lead to scam or malware sites.
adjudications.rationale: An unsolicited SMS from an unknown number containing only a teaser and an obfuscated random .sbs domain is a classic smishing payload; the workers split medium/high but the strongest defensible reading is high.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. If you choose to participate, confirm the link stays on terac.com before signing in.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a genuine paid-task invitation from Terac, the platform running this study. It is safe; just make sure any link you open stays on the official terac.com site.
adjudications.rationale: Authenticated from notifications.terac.com (SPF and DMARC pass) with all links on terac.com and no request for credentials or payment; this is the real platform's own invitation, so safe/not_scam is correct despite mild marketing urgency.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. Use your referral link only on the official terac.com site.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a real referral notice from Terac, the platform running this study. It is safe to ignore or to use as intended.
adjudications.rationale: Authenticated from notifications.terac.com with links on terac.com and no sensitive request; a genuine platform notification, so safe/not_scam.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. Open any referral link only on the official terac.com site.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a genuine referral-opportunity email from Terac, the platform running this study. It is safe.
adjudications.rationale: Same authenticated Terac referral template (SPF/DMARC pass, terac.com links, no credential or payment request); safe/not_scam is the defensible final target.

adjudications.recommended_action: If you want to take part, log in through the official terac.com site rather than trusting any embedded link.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This matches Terac's genuine paid-interview invitation. It is most likely legitimate; to be safe, reach Terac through its official website rather than email links.
adjudications.rationale: Content is the standard Terac paid-interview template, but this copy carries no sender-domain or authentication metadata to confirm it, so low (benign content, sender unverifiable) is more defensible than a clean safe.

adjudications.recommended_action: Do not click the link. Check any real delivery directly in the official courier app or website. Delete the message.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a fake parcel-delivery text. The 'parcel' brand and the courier name don't match, and the link is a trap. Never confirm deliveries through links in texts like this.
adjudications.rationale: Unsolicited delivery SMS naming a mismatched brand ('Joybuy' parcel via a DPD link) with a time-pressure hook is textbook delivery smishing; the single worker's high rating is correct and I sharpen the scam type to delivery_phishing.

adjudications.recommended_action: If interested, check the offer inside the official Bybit app rather than email links.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a genuine promotional email from the Bybit crypto exchange. It is not a scam, but only act on crypto offers from inside the official app.
adjudications.rationale: Authenticated from mail.bybit.com (SPF and DMARC pass) with links on Bybit's own domain; a legitimate exchange promotion, so low/not_scam with standard verify-in-app advice.

adjudications.recommended_action: If you want the offer, open it from inside the official Airtel app rather than the text link.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This looks like a real Airtel promotion, since the link points to Airtel's own site. To be safe, claim any offer from the official Airtel app instead of the text.
adjudications.rationale: The link resolves to i.airtel.in, Airtel's genuine domain, and the message offers a bundled service with no credential or payment request; low/not_scam is more defensible than the single worker's medium 'suspicious_link' guess.

adjudications.recommended_action: Do not click the links or reply. Delete the email.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This 'offer letter' is a job scam. A real employer doesn't send a surprise offer through a mass-mailer with no interview. Don't click the accept/reject links.
adjudications.rationale: An unsolicited 'offer letter' from clickearners.com routed through aweber mass-mailing links, with generic accept/reject prompts and boilerplate address, is a classic work-from-home / fake-job lure; the worker's high rating is correct and the precise type is fake_job.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. If booking, go to the cinema's official site or app rather than email links.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a normal cinema marketing newsletter advertising movies and loyalty perks. It is not a scam.
adjudications.rationale: Standard promotional newsletter from a real cinema chain (Ster-Kinekor) with loyalty/movie content and no sensitive request; benign marketing, so low/not_scam.

adjudications.recommended_action: If interested, independently verify the research company before joining, and never pay to take part or share financial details.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a paid market-research recruitment email. These are often legitimate, but confirm the company is real first and remember a genuine study never asks you to pay.
adjudications.rationale: A paid focus-group recruitment with a plausible incentive and no link or payment request reads as legitimate market research; low/not_scam with a verify-the-company caution is the defensible target.

adjudications.recommended_action: Be cautious before filling out the form. Do not provide financial or identity details, and verify who 'Fanburster' is independently.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This reply to a Craigslist ad pushes you to an outside Google Form with first-come urgency. Treat it carefully and never share payment or ID details to 'qualify'.
adjudications.rationale: An unsolicited Craigslist reply funnelling to an external Google Form with scarcity framing and a referral angle is a common low-grade gig/data-harvest scheme; medium is more defensible than the worker's low rating, though it stops short of confirmed fraud.

adjudications.recommended_action: If you want to object or claim, go to the official settlement site by searching for it yourself rather than clicking the email link.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a real legal notice about a class-action judgment against Google. It is informational and asks for nothing; just confirm any claim site independently.
adjudications.rationale: The notice tracks an actual federal class-action judgment against Google with specific, verifiable detail and makes no payment or credential request; low/not_scam, matching the worker's read.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. Register through the company's official site if you want to attend.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a normal product/event marketing email from GitLab. It is not a scam.
adjudications.rationale: A legitimate corporate event-invitation newsletter naming real companies and a real product launch, with no sensitive request; low/not_scam, correcting the worker's overcautious medium.

adjudications.recommended_action: Do not click anything or 'confirm billing details'. Delete it. TV Licensing never emails to release a refund this way; check your account by typing the official site yourself.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a refund phishing scam impersonating TV Licensing. The promised refund and the request to 'confirm billing details' are bait to steal your card and personal information. Do not respond.
adjudications.rationale: A surprise refund that demands you 'confirm billing information' to release funds is a hallmark refund/billing phishing pattern impersonating UK TV Licensing; the lone worker wrongly marked this safe, and expert adjudication corrects it to high government_impersonation.

adjudications.recommended_action: Do not send your resume or personal details yet. Verify the company and recruiter independently before engaging.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is an unsolicited recruiter email offering a vague gig and asking for your resume. Treat it with caution and confirm the company is real before sharing anything.
adjudications.rationale: An out-of-the-blue 'I came across your profile' recruiting pitch for a vague task that requests a resume is a common fake-job pattern; SPF/DMARC pass and the ask is mild, so medium fake_job (not high) is the defensible target, matching the worker's caution.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. Look up showtimes on the studio's or cinema's official site if interested.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a movie promotion from a film studio sent through a marketing service. It is not a scam.
adjudications.rationale: A Sony Pictures film-release promotion delivered via a standard email service provider with passing authentication and only marketing/unsubscribe links is benign; low/not_scam.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. Track the order in the official Amazon app if you want details.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is an ordinary order-shipped notice with no link to click and nothing to confirm. It is safe.
adjudications.rationale: A plain shipping notification containing no link, no urgency, and no request, pointing the user to the official app, is benign; safe/not_scam.

adjudications.recommended_action: Do not click the link or confirm any address. Delete it. Check real deliveries only in the official courier app.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a fake 'delivery delayed' message designed to scare you into clicking. The 24-hour deadline and the link are the scam. Never confirm your address through a link like this.
adjudications.rationale: A delayed-delivery notice demanding you 'confirm your address immediately' via a generic link under a 24-hour cancellation threat is textbook delivery phishing; high delivery_phishing.

adjudications.recommended_action: Verify the sender before clicking 'apply'. Confirm the staffing agency through its official site rather than the email link.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This shift-work job email could be from a real staffing agency, but the sender can't be confirmed and the 'apply' links are unverified. Check who sent it before clicking.
adjudications.rationale: An unsolicited shift-recruitment email with unverified 'click here to apply' links and no confirmable sender carries moderate fake-job risk; medium with a verify-sender caution is more defensible than the worker's 'unsure'.

adjudications.recommended_action: Verify the sender before clicking 'apply'. Confirm the staffing agency through its official site rather than the email link.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This shift-work job email could be from a real staffing agency, but the sender can't be confirmed and the 'apply' links are unverified. Check who sent it before clicking.
adjudications.rationale: Identical shift-recruitment content to the companion submission, with unverified apply links and no confirmable sender; adjudicated consistently as medium fake_job.

adjudications.recommended_action: If interested in the EMI offer, open it inside the official ICICI Bank app or website rather than email links.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This looks like a normal promotional email from ICICI Bank advertising No-Cost EMI. It is not a scam, but act on bank offers only through the official app.
adjudications.rationale: Bank marketing copy that explicitly redirects to the official ICICI app/website with no credential or payment request reads as legitimate promotion; low/not_scam with verify-in-app advice.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. This is an informational government newsletter; ignore or unsubscribe if unwanted.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a routine government magazine newsletter from an Indian police-research bureau. It is informational and not a scam.
adjudications.rationale: A bilingual government magazine circular sent via an official gov.in channel, with only an unsubscribe action, is benign informational mail; low/not_scam.

adjudications.recommended_action: Treat immigration-consultancy marketing with care. Never pay upfront fees and verify any consultant against official government immigration sources.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is lead-generation marketing from an immigration consultancy promising fast results. It is not outright fraud, but be cautious — check official government sites and avoid upfront fees.
adjudications.rationale: Immigration-consultancy content offering '60-second eligibility' and fast PR is lead-generation marketing that frequently shades into upfront-fee schemes; low/other with a do-not-pay caution is defensible without alleging confirmed fraud.

adjudications.recommended_action: If interested, apply through the official Naukri site and independently verify the hiring company before sharing details.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a normal job-board notification from Naukri inviting you to apply. It is generally legitimate; still verify the employer before sharing personal information.
adjudications.rationale: A standard job-portal (Naukri) invitation with the portal's own authenticity disclaimer and a detailed real-looking role is benign; low/not_scam with a verify-the-employer caution.

adjudications.recommended_action: Do not click the links or claim the bonus. Be skeptical of 'free credit' trading promotions.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This pushes a crypto/prediction-market 'sign up and get $20 free' offer through a personal-looking address and tracking links. 'Free trading credit' promotions are a common hook — don't click.
adjudications.rationale: A mass 'free $20 trading bonus' crypto/prediction-market promotion sent from a personal outlook.com address via third-party tracking links combines too-good-to-be-true bait with a mismatched sender; medium crypto is the defensible level.

adjudications.recommended_action: No action needed. Remember that leveraged CFD/crypto trading carries real financial risk; only act inside the official Bitget app.
adjudications.plain_english_warning: This is a genuine market-commentary newsletter from the Bitget crypto exchange. It is not a scam, but the leveraged trading it promotes is financially risky.
adjudications.rationale: A long market-analysis newsletter from Bitget's authenticated promo domain (SPF/DMARC pass) with no credential or payment request is a legitimate exchange mailing; the from/reply-to difference is normal promo-vs-corporate domain use, so low/not_scam with a trading-risk caution.
